rationale: Title X is stronger for HR because it states the method, clinical use, study design, and the key quantitative cutoff. The 300-Hz cutoff and prospective mapping detail make it more informative and publication-ready, despite being slightly longer.
loser_weakness: Title Y is clear but generic. It omits the study design, the cutoff, and the main quantitative specificity, so it reads more like a broad concept than a precise manuscript title.
